title: retama sphaerocarpa var. atlantica
intro: Retama sphaerocarpa var. atlantica, also known as the North African broom or Atlas broom, is a plant species that belongs to the pea family and is native to North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antifungal properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems such as bronchitis and asthma. Retama sphaerocarpa var. atlantica can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used in traditional herbal medicine to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
